input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's the full setup:

**AppShell.xaml**
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyApp.Views"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
       Title="MyApp">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, shown directly from the flyout -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — two tabs inside one flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent Title="Active"
                          ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent Title="Archived"
                          ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**
```csharp
namespace MyApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register the detail page so you can navigate with:
        //   await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
        //       $"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Views.ProductDetailsPage),
                              typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Key points:**

- Each `FlyoutItem` is one entry in the flyout menu.
- Wrapping two `Tab` elements inside the Products `FlyoutItem` gives you the bottom tab bar with "Active" and "Archived".
- `ProductDetailsPage` is registered as a *global route* — it isn't in the visual hierarchy, so it pushes onto the navigation stack from any page via `GoToAsync`.
- Accept query parameters in `ProductDetailsPage` with `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]` or the `IQueryAttributable` interface.
